message:
feat(recorder_migration): Cx-first m0_preflight (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Begin Cx-first migration of recorder_migration.rs. MigrationEngine
had 0 of 5 public async entry points with cx siblings despite
driving long-running storage scans on large event ledgers.

Addition (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `MigrationEngine::m0_preflight_with_cx(cx, source_storage,
    source_reader)` — Cx-first parallel of `m0_preflight`.
    Checkpoints at 3 seams:
      - pre-flight: pre-cancelled cx returns early with
        SourceDegraded error wrapping the cancellation reason.
      - between health check and head offset read: a cancel
        here skips the reader.head_offset() call when the
        result will be discarded.
      - per-batch inside the scan loop: cx.checkpoint()? at the
        top of each cursor.next_batch iteration with the running
        event_count and last_ordinal embedded in the
        cancellation error. This is the critical seam — on
        large ledgers the scan can take minutes, and without
        per-batch cancellation the caller can't abort.

Cancellation error routing: cancellation is mapped onto
`MigrationError::SourceDegraded { last_error: Some(...) }`. This
is an imperfect fit (the source isn't actually degraded; the
caller cancelled) but preserves the `Result<_, MigrationError>`
signature so callers don't need to handle a new error variant.
The message explicitly starts with "cancelled" so callers can
pattern-match on it if needed.

Regression tests (2 new):

  * `m0_preflight_with_cx_matches_legacy` — runs both variants
    against identical 5-record fixtures (2 panes, ordinals 0-4)
    and asserts bit-for-bit manifest parity across event_count,
    first_ordinal, last_ordinal, per_pane_counts, last_offset.

  * `m0_preflight_with_cx_rejects_degraded_source` — verifies
    the health-check branch still short-circuits on the Cx
    path, surfacing a SourceDegraded error containing
    "degraded". Guards against a regression that could
    accidentally skip the health check when the pre-flight
    checkpoint fires on an uncancelled cx.

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * recorder_migration:: — 50 passed / 0 failed (tick 66: 48 ->
    tick 67: 50, +2 new parity tests; all 3 legacy m0 tests
    still green)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (51.19s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Cx-first recorder_migration.rs progress after tick 67:
  * `m0_preflight`       ✓ (tick 67, this commit)
  * `m1_export`          — synchronous, no cx needed
  * `m2_import`          — pending
  * `m3_checkpoint_sync` — pending
  * `m5_cutover`         — pending
  * `run_m0_m2`          — pending (composite)

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
